meta_title: Impact Windows in Century Village, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your Century Village home with hurricane-rated impact windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs ESW & CWI windows in Palm Beach County. Financing from $87/mo. Call today!
h1: Impact Windows Built for Century Village Homes — Protection, Comfort & Lasting Value
intro:
Century Village in West Palm Beach is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved 55+ communities, home to thousands of residents who have built their lives and retirement years within its friendly, well-kept neighborhoods. The tree-lined streets, active Clubhouse, and tight-knit atmosphere make it a genuinely special place to call home. But beneath that comfort lies a real vulnerability: the vast majority of Century Village's homes — an estimated 80% — were built before 1994, predating Florida's modern hurricane construction standards. For homeowners in ZIP code 33409, that means aging single-pane windows and traditional shutters remain the primary line of defense against storms that have repeatedly proven they can reach this far inland.

Located approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Century Village sits well within Palm Beach County's 140 mph design wind zone. While the community is not classified as High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Florida Building Code still mandates that all new window and door installations meet rigorous hurricane-rated product standards. Storms like Hurricane Frances in 2004, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017 demonstrated clearly that distance from the water is no guarantee of safety. Wind-driven rain, flying debris, and sustained gusts caused widespread damage throughout West Palm Beach and its surrounding communities, and Century Village was no exception. For residents managing fixed retirement budgets and protecting homes with an average value of around $160,000, the financial and emotional stakes of storm damage are very real.

The good news is that modern impact windows represent one of the smartest, most cost-effective investments a Century Village homeowner can make. Unlike storm shutters that require physical effort to deploy before every storm — a challenge for many older adults — impact windows provide around-the-clock, year-round protection with absolutely no action required on your part. They reduce noise from nearby traffic, help maintain comfortable indoor temperatures while lowering energy bills, and can qualify you for significant homeowner's insurance discounts. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped more than 500 South Florida homeowners make this upgrade, and our team knows exactly what Century Village homes need.

section_why.h2: Why Century Village Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact window installation in a community like Century Village isn't a decision to take lightly. Many companies subcontract their installation work, meaning the crew that shows up at your door may have little accountability to the company whose name is on the truck. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we do things differently. Every installation is performed 100% in-house by our own trained, background-checked technicians — never subcontracted. That direct accountability is a core part of why we've earned our reputation as 'The Kings of Service' across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience to every project. We understand the specific construction characteristics of the older homes found throughout Century Village, including the window frame configurations, wall compositions, and structural considerations common in pre-1994 builds. Upgrading an older home to meet modern Florida Building Code standards requires more than just swapping out glass — it requires a team that understands how those homes were built and what proper integration looks like. We navigate that process on your behalf, including pulling all required permits through the Palm Beach County Building Division, scheduling inspections, and ensuring every installation meets or exceeds code before we consider the job complete.

We're also deeply committed to transparent, honest communication with every homeowner. We know that for many Century Village residents on fixed incomes, this is a major decision. Our team will walk you through every product option, explain the differences in glass types and frame systems, and provide a clear, itemized quote with no hidden fees. We've maintained an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau because we treat every customer the way we'd want our own family treated. From the initial consultation at your home near the Century Village Clubhouse to the final walk-through after installation, we're with you every step of the way.

Century Village is a community built on trust, and so is Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. When neighbors talk to neighbors about home improvement — and in Century Village, they absolutely do — we want our name to come up as the company that delivered exceptional quality, kept its promises, and made the whole process simple and stress-free.

section_history.h2: Decades of Storms Remind Us Why Century Village Homes Need Better Protection
section_history.body:
Palm Beach County has experienced no fewer than 7 named storm events since 1990, and Century Village residents have felt the consequences of several of them firsthand. Hurricane Frances struck in September 2004, battering Palm Beach County with sustained winds and prolonged rain that caused widespread roof and window damage. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma roared through in October 2005 — one of the fastest-moving and most intensely destructive storms in Florida history — leaving a trail of shattered windows, damaged carports, and flooded interiors across West Palm Beach neighborhoods including Century Village. More recently, Hurricane Irma's 2017 track brought strong gusts and debris-laden winds through the region, reminding residents that these storms don't need a direct hit to cause serious harm.

What these storms reveal is a pattern that matters specifically for Century Village: older single-pane windows fail not only from direct wind pressure but from windborne debris impact. A single piece of airborne material — a broken tree branch, a piece of roofing tile, a section of a neighbor's fence — traveling at storm speeds can shatter a standard window in an instant, instantly compromising the structural integrity of the entire home and allowing destructive internal pressurization. Homes built before 1994 were not designed with today's understanding of how hurricane forces interact with a structure. That's precisely why modern impact windows are so critical for this community.

The century Village community's storm history is not just a set of statistics — it's a lived experience for many long-time residents who remember boarding up before Frances and cleaning up after Wilma. Impact windows eliminate that cycle of preparation, anxiety, and recovery, replacing it with permanent, passive protection that works whether you're home or away visiting family in another state.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Century Village falls under Palm Beach County's jurisdiction for all building permits and construction inspections, which means any window replacement project must comply with both the Florida Building Code (FBC) and Palm Beach County's local amendments. While Century Village is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily covering Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — that does not mean standard windows are acceptable. The Florida Building Code still requires that all replacement windows in Palm Beach County's 140 mph design wind zone be tested and rated to resist hurricane-force conditions. Products must carry valid product approval numbers under Florida's statewide approval system, and all installations must be permitted through the Palm Beach County Building Division.

For pre-1994 homes — which make up approximately 80% of Century Village's housing stock — the permitting process is especially important. These older homes may require buck framing, structural reinforcement around rough openings, or updated anchoring methods to ensure the new impact windows are installed in full compliance with current code. Our team handles every aspect of this process, from submitting permit applications with the Palm Beach County Building Division to scheduling and passing all required inspections. We never skip this step. Unpermitted window work not only violates Florida law but can void your homeowner's insurance coverage and create complications when you sell your home.

Every window we install carries the required Florida Product Approval documentation, which confirms it has been independently tested to perform under the wind loads specified for your zone. This documentation is essential for your wind mitigation inspection report, which in turn is the primary document your insurance company uses to calculate any discounts you may qualify for. We provide homeowners with complete permit and product documentation packages upon project completion so you have everything you need, organized and ready to submit to your insurer.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI Impact Products Built for Century Village Conditions
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we install two trusted, Florida-approved impact window brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are purpose-built for South Florida's demanding climate conditions and carry the product approvals necessary for installation throughout Palm Beach County. For Century Village homeowners, these products represent a significant upgrade not just in storm protection, but in everyday quality of life.

ESW impact windows feature laminated glass constructed with a tough interlayer that holds the glass together upon impact rather than allowing it to shatter inward. This lamination technology is the same principle used in automobile windshields, and it means that even if the glass cracks under extreme force, the window continues to function as a barrier against wind and water intrusion. ESW frames are engineered with multi-chamber designs that improve structural rigidity while also providing superior thermal insulation compared to older single-chamber aluminum frames common in Century Village's pre-1994 homes. The addition of Low-E (low emissivity) glass coatings further reduces heat transfer, meaning your air conditioner works less hard to maintain a comfortable indoor temperature — a meaningful benefit in Florida's climate and a real reduction in monthly utility costs.

CWI products complement our ESW offerings with a range of styles and configurations suited to the varied window openings found across Century Village's housing units, including single-hung, double-hung, casement, and horizontal sliding options. Whether your home has standard-sized openings or the more unusual configurations sometimes found in older construction, we carry and install products that fit properly and perform to code. Both ESW and CWI windows are available in finishes and grid patterns that complement Century Village's classic Florida architectural aesthetic, ensuring your home looks as good as it's protected.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost for Century Village Homes — And How to Make Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Century Village, where the average property value sits around $160,000, understanding the cost of impact windows in the context of your overall investment is essential. The typical cost for a complete impact window replacement in a Century Village home — which generally ranges from 6 to 12 windows depending on the unit size — runs between $8,000 and $18,000, with exact pricing depending on window count, size, style, and any structural work required at older openings. While that may seem significant on a fixed income, the return on that investment through energy savings, insurance discounts, and avoided storm damage costs makes it one of the highest-value home improvements available in South Florida.

To make impact windows genuinely accessible for Century Village residents, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down required for qualified buyers. Our financing partners offer a range of terms designed to fit different budget situations, and our team will work with you to find a payment structure that makes sense for your monthly expenses. For many homeowners, the monthly financing payment is partially or fully offset by reductions in their homeowner's insurance premium — meaning the upgrade can be essentially cost-neutral from a cash-flow perspective.

From a pure return-on-investment standpoint, impact windows in Palm Beach County typically deliver several compelling financial benefits: reduced cooling costs (often 10-25% lower energy bills due to Low-E glass and improved frame insulation), annual homeowner's insurance savings that in some cases exceed $500 per year, and increased resale value. In a community like Century Village where resale is an important consideration for many residents, having fully permitted, code-compliant impact windows already installed is a meaningful selling point — buyers know they don't need to budget for that upgrade themselves.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums with Impact Windows in Century Village
section_insurance.body:
One of the most tangible financial benefits of upgrading to impact windows in Century Village is the effect on your homeowner's insurance premium. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts for homes with verified wind mitigation features, and impact windows are among the most heavily credited upgrades under the state's wind mitigation inspection system. After your installation is complete, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your new windows and issue a Wind Mitigation Report — this report is submitted to your insurance carrier and can result in premium reductions of 15% to 35% or more depending on your current policy and carrier.

Many Century Village homeowners carry policies with insurers active in Palm Beach County, including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation — Florida's state-backed insurer of last resort — as well as private carriers like Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others. Each of these carriers recognizes wind mitigation credits for properly installed, Florida-approved impact windows. The credits apply specifically because impact windows eliminate one of the most common pathways for hurricane damage: opening failure. When windows hold, internal pressurization is avoided, and the structural integrity of the entire home is far more likely to be maintained throughout a storm event.

For Century Village residents on fixed incomes and retirement budgets, those annual insurance savings are not abstract — they're real dollars that stay in your pocket every year for the life of the windows. Combined with reduced energy bills and the elimination of storm shutter storage and deployment costs, the total annual savings can be substantial. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will connect you with a trusted wind mitigation inspector after your installation so you can submit your updated report to your insurer and start benefiting from lower premiums as quickly as possible.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Century Village if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Century Village is located approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coast, but it sits squarely within Palm Beach County's 140 mph design wind zone. Storms like Hurricane Frances, Wilma, and Irma all caused significant damage throughout the West Palm Beach area. Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated windows throughout the county regardless of coastal proximity, and the protection impact windows provide against windborne debris is just as critical in Century Village as it is closer to the water.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost, and what financing options are available for Century Village homeowners?
faqs.a: For a typical Century Village home, a full impact window replacement generally ranges from $8,000 to $18,000 depending on the number of windows, their sizes, and any structural work required at older openings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down for qualified buyers. Many homeowners find that monthly financing costs are partially offset by insurance premium savings and reduced energy bills, making the upgrade surprisingly affordable on a monthly cash-flow basis.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Century Village, and do you handle that process?
faqs.a: All window replacement projects in Century Village require permits pulled through the Palm Beach County Building Division, along with inspections to confirm code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — we prepare and submit the application, provide all required Florida Product Approval documentation for the windows we install, and coordinate all required inspections. You don't need to navigate the county permitting system yourself; we manage it completely as part of our installation service.

faqs.q: How long does the impact window installation process take in Century Village?
faqs.a: For most Century Village homes, the physical window installation takes one to two days depending on the number of windows and complexity of the project. The overall timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically runs three to six weeks, accounting for permit processing time through the Palm Beach County Building Division and product ordering. Our team communicates with you throughout the process so you always know where your project stands and when to expect our crew on-site.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance by installing impact windows in my Century Village home?
faqs.a: Insurance savings vary by carrier and current policy, but homeowners in Palm Beach County with properly documented impact windows commonly see premium reductions of 15% to 35% or more. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your new windows and issue a Wind Mitigation Report, which you submit to your insurer to claim applicable credits. For many Century Village homeowners, these annual savings alone represent a meaningful return on investment, and they compound every year for the life of the windows.
